retrieved_docs.title: Oral Surgery, Extraction of Roots -- Technique or Treatment
retrieved_docs.content: Another modern advancement in atraumatic tooth extraction is Piezosurgery. This cutting system creates osteotomies through ultrasonic vibrations, which promotes cleaner surgical cuts compared to oscillating saws or surgical burs. Piezosurgery permits the surgeon to remove only the necessary bone to facilitate root extraction. It also does not cause harm to soft tissues or neurovascular structures, unlike surgical burs or oscillating saws that obliterate hard and soft tissue indiscriminately. [20] Surgical skill comes with knowledge and consistent practice. As the surgeon gains experience in exodontia, they will be able to feel when their application of force is appropriate, promoting efficiency and safety during the surgery.

retrieved_docs.title: Ultrasound Therapy -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Engineering advancements and dedicated research have paved the way for FDA approval of ultrasound therapy in various disciplines. Overall, ultrasound therapy can be divided  into "low" and "high" power therapies. [13] High power - Lithotripsy and HIFU Low Power - bone healing, sonophoresis, and diathermy

retrieved_docs.title: Alveolar Osteitis -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Recent evidence suggests that phototherapy, specifically low-level laser therapy (LLLT), has a role in symptom management. It is easy to use, quick, relatively safe, and financially advantageous. The results are comparable to all the alternative management strategies above, and many studies find the results superior. Photobiomodulation therapy has been shown to have a higher ability to decrease pain and is considered by some to be the most effective intervention available. [12] Low-level laser therapy (LLLT) is a newer technology, and further evaluation is needed, but it could become a mainstay of treatment in the future.

retrieved_docs.title: Cuboid Stress Fractures -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Bone stimulators have achieved attention in the last few years. There are currently two types of devices on the market. The first type uses electromagnetic energy that generates magnetic fields over the fracture site. The premise is that this energy can open calcium channels in cell membranes, which increase calmodulin, thus increasing cell proliferation and healing. However, no conclusive data demonstrate that electromagnetic bone stimulators enhance healing. [8] The second type is a pulsed ultrasound device that is theorized to increase vascular endothelial growth factor and fibroblast growth factor, which can promote angiogenesis. Unfortunately, literature pertaining specifically to pulsed ultrasound is limited. There was a small military study that looked at 43 tibial shaft fractures. This study concluded there was no significant difference in time to healing by adding a pulsed ultrasound bone stimulator to the usual treatment regimen of rest and activity modification. [8]

retrieved_docs.content: Sound energy is created by disturbances in the density of particles in air, an elastic medium that allows sound to travel. The speed of sound is slower in air than in fluids in the inner ear. Repetitive patterns produce musical tones, whereas sound that lacks these patterns is perceived as noise.

retrieved_docs.title: Craniofacial Distraction Osteogenesis -- Technique or Treatment
retrieved_docs.content: Latency Phase: After osteotomy and device placement, the device is not activated for a period of 24 hours to five days, depending on the site of the desired distraction. This latency period allows for bone healing to begin by way of the formation of a soft callus.

retrieved_docs.title: Mandible Reconstruction -- Technique or Treatment
retrieved_docs.content: Non-vascularized bone grafts may be harvested from many sites throughout the body and commonly include the iliac crest and anterior tibia. [26] In the iliac crest harvest, an incision is made in the skin lateral to the anterior superior iliac spine (ASIS). The soft tissue is rolled medially, and dissection is continued with monopolar electrocautery (settings of 40-40) to reveal the ASIS's surface. A #9 elevator or similar instrument is then used to lift the periosteum from the medial aspect of the superior ilium; the surrounding muscles are then retracted medially. A reciprocating saw is used under copious saline irrigation to cut a rectangle of cortical bone of whatever size is desired. The deep aspect of the bone can be released with an osteotome and mallet. After removal, additional bone can be scraped from the wound bed with a curette, if necessary. Harvest from the anterior tibia and other bone sites is similar; a skin incision facilitates access to the bone, and a monocortical section is removed, leaving the bulk of the bone intact and not compromising the donor bone's load-bearing ability. The harvested bone can be used as a single strip if desired, but for mandible reconstruction, the bone is often passed through a bone mill to create medium-to-fine sized chunks of fresh bone. These fresh bone chips are then packed into a tray to fill the mandibular defect. In some cases, harvested bone can be mixed with allograft bone to augment the available volume.

retrieved_docs.title: Sonography Physical Principles And Instrumentation -- Definition/Introduction -- Instrumentation
retrieved_docs.content: The linear transducer does not directly utilize every crystal, but each element is electrically stimulated or vibrated and subsequently excites adjacent crystals. Each crystal within the linear transducer is not given direct electrical stimulation. The advantage of the linear probe is a larger field of view and a higher superficial focus. Common clinical applications of this probe are within the soft tissue and more superficial structures. See Image. Ultrasound Linear Probe.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 3 -- Community 2204
retrieved_docs.content: Other OR therapeutic procedures on musculoskeletal system is a classification term that categorizes other or therapeutic procedures on the musculoskeletal system, grouping together various interventions aimed at treating musculoskeletal conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2804
retrieved_docs.content: Ultrazvuk, which denotes ultrasound, is directly linked with المعالجة بموجات فوق صوتية, the Arabic term for ultrasound wave therapy, indicating that ultrasound technology is employed as a therapeutic modality in medical treatment.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 1853
retrieved_docs.content: Other OR therapeutic procedures on joints is a classification that categorizes other or therapeutic procedures on joints.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 32 -- Level 4 -- Community 2875
retrieved_docs.content: Musculoskeletal disorder therapy is closely linked to the use of ultrasound therapy (المعالجة بموجات فوق صوتية). The modality of ultrasound (ultrazvuk) functions as an entry combination for this therapy, indicating that basic ultrasound application is incorporated into the treatment approach. Additionally, ultrasound surgery (ultrazvuková chirurgie) is related to the same ultrasound therapy, reflecting that therapeutic ultrasound can also be employed in surgical procedures. Together, these connections underscore the central role of ultrasound‑based techniques in managing musculoskeletal disorders, both as a non‑invasive therapeutic modality and as a component of surgical intervention.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 675

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1930
retrieved_docs.content: The term “Other OR therapeutic procedures on musculoskeletal system” functions as a classification label for the broader category “other or therapeutic procedures on musculoskeletal system.” Within this classified group, the specific intervention “Reopening of osteotomy site of facial bone” is regarded as a related and possibly synonymous procedure, indicating that it belongs to the same set of musculoskeletal therapeutic actions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 4 -- Community 2779
retrieved_docs.content: Ultrazvuk and ultrazvuková chirurgie are both directly linked to المعالجة بموجات فوق صوتية, indicating that conventional ultrasound imaging as well as ultrasound‑guided surgical procedures are utilized within the context of treatment with ultrasound waves. This relationship underscores the role of ultrasound technology both as a diagnostic modality (ultrazvuk) and as a therapeutic tool in surgical interventions (ultrazvuková chirurgie) when delivering المعالجة بموجات فوق صوتية.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 4 -- Community 2822
retrieved_docs.content: Ultrazvuková chirurgie (ultrasound surgery) is classified as a type of جراحة (surgery). Surgery itself is a subcategory of الإجراءات العلاجية، غير مصنف في موضع آخر (therapeutic procedures, not classified elsewhere), placing ultrasound surgery within the broader hierarchy of unclassified therapeutic procedures. In addition to this hierarchical relationship, ultrazvuková chirurgie is closely linked to المعالجة بموجات فوق صوتية (ultrasound therapy), reflecting the shared use of ultrasound energy in both surgical and non‑surgical therapeutic contexts. Although الإجراءات العلاجية، غير مصنف في موضع آخر is related to المعالجة بموجات فوق صوتية, the primary structure positions surgery and its ultrasound‑based variant under the unclassified therapeutic procedures category, with ultrasound therapy serving as a related modality rather than a direct parent. This integrated view highlights how ultrasound‑based techniques span both surgical and therapeutic domains, informing clinical categorization and patient‑care strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 3 -- Community 1425
retrieved_docs.content: and ultrasound surgery (ultrazvuková chirurgie) also has a direct relationship with ultrasound therapy, emphasizing the centrality of ultrasonic technology in both diagnostic and therapeutic surgical contexts. Overall, the knowledge network shows that surgery, ultrasound‑based interventions, exercise‑based therapies, and rehabilitative approaches are interwoven, with many procedures and techniques classified under multiple parent categories. This integrated view supports a comprehensive approach to patient care, where clinicians can select among surgical, ultrasonic, exercise, and rehabilitation modalities—often in combination—to address a wide range of therapeutic needs.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 2 -- Community 554
retrieved_docs.content: diagnostics, cardiac surgical interventions, ultrasound‑guided and other minimally invasive therapies, and exercise‑based rehabilitative modalities, emphasizing an integrated approach to diagnosis, treatment, and patient education.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 21 -- Level 3 -- Community 1928
retrieved_docs.content: The procedure known as المعالجة بموجات فوق صوتية (ultrasound therapy) is classified as a type of other therapeutic procedures. In terminology resources, the Czech term ultrazvuk is listed as an entry combination for المعالجة بموجات فوق صوتية, indicating that ultrazvuk refers to the same therapeutic modality. Additionally, the Czech phrase ultrazvuková chirurgie (ultrasound surgery) is described as having a relationship with المعالجة بموجات فوق صوتية, reflecting the close connection between therapeutic ultrasound applications and surgical techniques that employ ultrasound energy. Recognizing these classifications and linguistic links helps integrate multilingual medical information and supports accurate identification of ultrasound‑based treatments within broader therapeutic categories.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 4 -- Community 2885
retrieved_docs.content: Ultrazvuk (ultrasound) is recognized as an entry combination that forms part of المعالجة بموجات فوق صوتية (ultrasound therapy). المعالجة بموجات فوق صوتية is organized under the broader category الإجراءات العلاجية، غير مصنف في موضع آخر (therapeutic procedures, not classified elsewhere), indicating that ultrasound therapy is considered a specific type of therapeutic procedure within this general classification.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 4 -- Community 2840
retrieved_docs.content: Ultrazvuková chirurgie is classified as a sub‑type of جراحة, indicating that ultrasound‑guided surgical techniques fall under the broader category of surgery. Within the scope of جراحة, there is a clinical association with عمليات الأنف, meaning that nasal surgical procedures are recognized as part of the clinical applications of surgery. This relationship highlights that both conventional and ultrasound‑assisted surgical approaches are relevant to the management of conditions involving the nose.
